must_detect.matched_text:
On 2026‑05‑20, an operational misconfiguration in Atlas HR Onboard v4 caused one onboarding form to be queued for a cross‑border transfer to our Singapore processor, Nimbus Apex Data Pte. Ltd., during setup of the M&A clean room for the proposed acquisition of Orchid Reef Systems Limited by Harbor Frontier Capital Fund I LP.
The queued dataset was limited by minimisation controls to name initials, corporate email, job title, and start month; no MAC addresses, device IMEI, GPS, face images, or ID card scans were collected or exported.
Under PDPO, the record constituted personal data; the transfer pipeline executed once at 02:14 HKT and moved a single file “Onboard_Form_v3 (scrn).pdf” (hash: 9f:2a:hk:…:77) to a staging bucket outside HK before automatic quarantine triggered.
Consent withdrawal: Mr. Adrian Lau (Emp Num: E3  7  9*HK) lodged withdrawal of consent for overseas payroll processing on 2026‑05‑18; his data was excluded from the export by rule “CBT‑EX‑HR‑MIN‑04,” and logs confirm zero fields for his profile were transmitted.
DSAR: On 2026‑05‑19, we received a data subject access request from Ms. Yvette Ho (ex‑analyst, DR ref: DSAR‑2026‑HK‑1z) seeking copies of HR processing records; acknowledgment was sent within 2 business days and the dataset was delivered on 2026‑05‑21 with redactions applied per PDPO s.20(2).
Erasure: The single staged file was remotely purged from SG at 09:10 HKT on 2026‑05‑20 with deletion certificate NAD‑DEL‑2026‑051; local cache copies (\\sjg‑dataroom‑01\m&a\acq_orchid\DR\) were wiped via secure erase v2, completed 2026‑05‑20 10:03 HKT.
Retention: Access logs retained for 90 days under Schedule R.90 and then aggregated; personal identifiers in logs are masked (e.g., A**n L**), and any residual raw events are set to auto‑delete at day 91.
DPA schedule: Nimbus Apex operates under DPA Schedule 2 (Subprocessors) and Schedule 4 (Cross‑Border Transfer Safeguards); a data‑export approval (Permit CBT‑HK‑2026‑05‑18) exists for fund operations, but onboarding HR forms are out of scope and the permit was revoked post‑incident.
We reviewed SFO implications: no MNPI concerning Seaport Jade Group Limited or Orchid Reef Systems Limited was included; the dataset did not contain financial forecasts, deal terms, or inside information likely to be price‑sensitive under Part X I V A SFO.
HKEX Listing Rules consideration (H K E X  –  L R 13.09): given the limited scope, prompt containment, and absence of market‑sensitive content, we determined no disclosure obligation arose; SFC informal consultation (tel. log ref: SFC‑IC‑2026‑05‑22) noted no further action.
Public‑source content in the clean room (board biographies from the target’s website) was present but is non‑confidential; the phrase “transfer all HR records” appears only in a deprecated policy excerpt and did not occur in fact.
